findings: Features Bwd Packet Length Mean and Avg Bwd Segment Size exhibit perfect correlation but both show high class separation individually, indicating redundancy coexists with apparent importance.
Strongly correlated feature pairs form stable dependency clusters across partitions without evidence of unstable dependencies.
Distributional profiles confirm that correlated features maintain discriminative power across classes, supporting the unresolved tension between redundancy and importance.
No contradictory or unstable dependency evidence was found in the local context.
limitations: Analysis limited to locally observed correlated features and available distribution verifications; broader global dependency shifts not assessed.
